justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name with broken status. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalations routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability correctly noted as adequate foundation (logging adequate, metrics adequate with specific gaps noted), distinguishing the infrastructure blocker from otherwise reasonable observability.

observations.text: Exemplary escalation structure — separated infrastructure gaps (queues, env vars) into distinct escalation entries with clear reasoning and correct routing to Coach K
new_infrastructure_required section is thorough, including not just the required items but also config_changes and infra_provisioning prerequisites
Correctly identified spec deviation as a separate escalation routed to Coach K → Bird/MJ, showing nuanced understanding of team roles
Logging coverage correctly marked adequate — no false positives on observability despite the overall NOT READY verdict
Resilience assessment goes beyond the rubric requirements, identifying failure modes like duplicate delivery and missing circuit breakers

Third escalation routes to 'Coach K → Bird (spec compliance) and MJ (architecture sign-off)' — while Coach K is the primary recipient, mentioning Bird and MJ by name could be seen as partial routing to other agents, though the primary routing is correct
Metrics coverage marked 'adequate' despite listing two concrete gaps (DLQ depth, consumer lag) — should arguably be 'gaps' rather than 'adequate' for consistency with the tracing assessment
